nonBlockingIssues.description: Commit message typo: says '9 unsafe blocks' but actual count is 11 (4+2 in interrupt_controller.rs + 5 in timer.rs). Handoff correctly reports 11.
Test count discrepancy: handoff claims 10 new tests but only 9 test functions exist. Existing count also off by one. Total of 24 is correct.
Test comment for parse_prop_length_overflow_returns_none says 'wraps on 64-bit' but u32→usize promotion cannot wrap on 64-bit. Code behavior is correct, just comment is misleading.
Minor test gap: no test covers FDT_PROP with nameoff pointing beyond the strings block. The code handles this safely (returns empty string), but an explicit test would strengthen coverage.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add to TDD section: when no bugs are found in a file, edge-case coverage tests that pass immediately are the expected output — TDD failing-first discipline only applies to actual bug findings. Also note that for hardware-coupled files like syscall.rs, TDD must use duplicated validation logic patterns (not direct kernel module imports) since kernel targets aarch64-unknown-none.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both device-serial-power-metrics-audit and syscall-audit workers noted this gap. AGENTS.md documents the import constraint but the skill file doesn't explain the duplication strategy for validation logic.
